content:
# John Chapter 5 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_005_page_001.jpg, JHN_005_page_002.jpg

### Healing at the Pool of Bethesda

1. Some time later, Jesus went up to Jerusalem for one of the Jewish festivals.

2. Inside the city, near the Sheep Gate, there was a pool called Bethesda, with five covered walkways surrounding it.

3. Crowds of sick people — blind, lame, paralyzed — lay on the walkways.

4. [This verse does not appear in the earliest manuscripts.]

5. One of the men lying there had been sick for thirty-eight years.

6. When Jesus saw him and knew he had been ill for a long time, he asked him, "Would you like to get well?"

7. "I can't, sir," the sick man said, "because I have no one to put me into the pool when the water bubbles up. By the time I get there, someone else has already gone in ahead of me."

8. Jesus told him, "Stand up, pick up your mat, and walk!"

9. Instantly, the man was healed! He rolled up his mat and began walking. But this miracle happened on the Sabbath,

10. so the Jewish leaders objected. They said to the man who was cured, "You can't carry that mat! It's the Sabbath — the law doesn't allow it."

11. But he replied, "The man who healed me told me, 'Pick up your mat and walk.'"

12. "Who said such a thing as that?" they demanded.

13. The man didn't know, because Jesus had slipped away into the crowd.

14. Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, "Now you are well. Stop sinning, or something even worse may happen to you."

15. The man went and told the Jewish leaders that it was Jesus who had healed him.

### Jesus Claims Divine Authority

16. So the Jewish leaders began harassing Jesus for breaking the Sabbath rules.

17. But Jesus replied, "My Father is always working, and so am I."

18. This made them all the more determined to kill him. Not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but he was calling God his own Father, making himself equal with God.

19. So Jesus explained, "I tell you the solemn truth: the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does.

20. For the Father loves the Son and shows him everything he does. And the Father will show him even greater works than the healing of this man, so that you will be amazed.

21. For just as the Father gives life to those he raises from the dead, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants.

22. The Father doesn't judge anyone. Instead, he has given the Son absolute authority to judge,

23. so that everyone will honor the Son, just as they honor the Father. Anyone who does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent him.

24. I tell you the solemn truth: those who listen to my message and believe in God who sent me have eternal life. They will never be condemned for their sins, but they have already passed from death into life.

25. And I assure you that the time is coming — indeed it's here now — when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen will live.

26. The Father has life in himself, and he has granted that same life-giving power to the Son.

27. And he has given him authority to judge, because he is the Son of Man.

28. Don't be surprised! Indeed, the time is coming when all the dead in their graves will hear the voice of God's Son,

29. and they will rise again. Those who have done good will rise to experience eternal life, and those who have practiced evil will rise to experience judgment.

30. I can do nothing on my own. I judge as God tells me. Therefore, my judgment is just, because I carry out the will of the one who sent me, not my own will.

### Witnesses to Jesus

31. If I were to testify on my own behalf, my testimony would not be valid.

32. But someone else testifies in my favor, and I know his testimony about me is true.

33. In fact, you sent investigators to listen to John the Baptist, and his testimony was true.

34. Of course, I don't need human witnesses, but I say these things so you might be saved.

35. John was like a burning and shining lamp, and you were excited for a while about his message.

36. But I have a greater witness than John — my works. The Father gave me these works to accomplish, and they prove that the Father has sent me.

37. And the Father who sent me has testified about me himself. You have never heard his voice or seen him face to face,

38. and you do not have his message living in your hearts, because you refuse to believe me — the one he sent to you.

39. You search the Scriptures because you think they give you eternal life. But the Scriptures point to me!

40. Yet you refuse to come to me to receive this life.

41. I don't need human approval.

42. But I know you — I know that you don't have God's love in your hearts.

43. For I have come in my Father's name, and you have rejected me. Yet if others come in their own name, you gladly welcome them.

44. No wonder you can't believe! You gladly accept praise from each other, but you don't care about the praise that comes from the one who alone is God.

45. Yet it isn't I who will accuse you before the Father. Moses will accuse you! Yes, Moses, in whom you put your hopes.

46. If you really believed Moses, you would believe me, because he wrote about me.

47. But since you don't believe what he wrote, how will you believe what I say?"

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 4 — The absent verse:** The oldest manuscripts, including Sinaiticus, do not contain the explanation about an angel stirring the water. This was added by later copyists to explain why the man in v.7 talks about the water being "stirred up." The superstition about angelic healing of the pool was local folklore, not part of the original Gospel.

**Verse 6 — "Would you like to get well?":** This sounds like an absurd question to ask a man who's been sick for 38 years. But Jesus is asking something profound: Have you given up? Has your identity become your illness? Do you still want healing, or have you settled into being a victim? The man's answer is telling — he doesn't say "yes." He gives an excuse.

**Verse 17 — "My Father is always working, and so am I":** This is the most provocative claim Jesus has made so far. Jewish theology held that God continues to work on the Sabbath — giving life to newborns and taking life from the dying — because God's work of sustaining creation never stops. Jesus claims that same uninterrupted divine work for himself. The opponents understand perfectly: he's claiming equality with God (v.18).

**Verse 39 — "You search the Scriptures":** The great irony of the chapter. Jesus' opponents are Bible experts. They study Scripture obsessively. But they miss the point of the entire Bible — which is to point to Jesus. Scripture knowledge without personal encounter with the living Word produces not life but condemnation. The Bible is a signpost, not the destination.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
